data.body: The Wiedemann-Franz law is observed in many “normal” materials, but to call it a law of physics is a bit of cheat.  There is no fundamental law that prevents this behavior.  It is CERTAINLY interesting and beautiful physics, but as one sees so often in the popular literature, it’s a bit oversold.

data.body:
What movie did you watch too young?

Bird of Paradise (1951), back when creaky old films were shown on Sunday afternoons and they were the only thing to watch unless you were into religious programming.

I was too young to understand SFX and thought the actor had actually jumped into a volcano.
